Risk Title: SQL Injection in Health Records API
Threat Event: SQL injection attack on health records API
Vulnerability: Lack of input validation on health records API
Control Recommendations: 1. Implement strict input validation (whitelisting); 2. Use parameterized queries exclusively; 3. Deploy WAF with SQL injection detection; 4. Automated SQL injection testing in CI/CD

Risk Title: Cloud SQL Database Publicly Accessible (Public IP)
Threat Event: Database publicly exposed via configuration error
Vulnerability: Cloud SQL database has public IP enabled
Current Controls: Firewall rules restrict access, but public IP exists
Control Recommendations: 1. Disable public IP on Cloud SQL instance; 2. Use private IP with VPC peering; 3. Organization Policy: Deny public IPs; 4. Weekly automated compliance scan

Risk Title: Insufficient Access Controls on ePHI (Overly Permissive IAM)
Vulnerability: Overly permissive IAM roles on Cloud SQL
Current Controls: Basic IAM roles, but no least-privilege enforcement
Control Recommendations: 1. Implement least-privilege IAM (roles, not individual permissions); 2. Quarterly access reviews; 3. MFA required for all Cloud SQL access; 4. Just-in-time (JIT) access for SRE team

Vulnerability: No DDoS protection beyond basic Cloud Armor
Current Controls: Cloud Armor with basic rate limiting
Control Recommendations: 1. Configure Cloud Armor advanced rules; 2. Deploy rate limiting at application layer; 3. Auto-scaling for GKE (HPA); 4. DDoS response playbook

Vulnerability: No MFA enforcement for all users
Current Controls: MFA available but not enforced for all users
Control Recommendations: 1. Enforce MFA for all users (Google Workspace, GCP, GitHub); 2. Deploy phishing-resistant MFA (WebAuthn/FIDO2); 3. Quarterly phishing simulation training; 4. Email security (DMARC, SPF, DKIM)

Risk Title: ePHI Leaked to Sentry Error Logs (Non-HIPAA Service)
Threat Event: ePHI leaked to Sentry error logs
Current Controls: Sentry before_send() hook with PHI scrubbing (not tested)
Control Recommendations: 1. Automated tests for Sentry PHI scrubbing (test_sentry_error_does_not_contain_phi); 2. CI/CD check: Fail build if Sentry event contains PHI patterns; 3. Developer training on PHI scrubbing; 4. Monthly audit of Sentry events for PHI leakage

Risk Title: Weak Session Timeout (30 min instead of 15 min)
Vulnerability: Weak session timeout (30 minutes instead of 15)
Control Recommendations: 1. Reduce session timeout to 15 minutes (HIPAA best practice); 2. Implement auto-logout on inactivity; 3. Clear session on browser close

Affected Assets: ASSET-004 (Cloud KMS), ASSET-001 (Cloud SQL), ASSET-003 (Cloud Storage)
Control Recommendations: 1. Upgrade to HSM-backed keys (FIPS 140-2 Level 3); 2. Alert on any Cloud KMS key access; 3. Implement key access approval workflow; 4. Test key rotation procedure

Current Controls: Daily backups with PITR, immutable backups (retention lock)
Control Recommendations: 1. Test backup restoration monthly (already planned); 2. Implement offline backup copies (air-gapped); 3. Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR); 4. Ransomware response playbook
1. Implement PHI detection in send_email() function; 2. Block emails containing PHI patterns; 3. Audit log all blocked emails; 4. Developer training on email PHI policy
1. Implement Pod Security Policies (or Pod Security Standards); 2. Restrict privileged containers; 3. Network policies to limit pod-to-pod communication; 4. Regular RBAC audits

Risk Title: Outdated Python Dependencies with Known Vulnerabilities
Control Recommendations: 1. Enforce dependency updates within 30 days of CVE; 2. Automated dependency scanning in CI/CD; 3. Block deployment if critical CVEs detected; 4. Quarterly dependency audit

Risk Title: Audit Logs Not Monitored for Anomalies
Vulnerability: Audit logs not monitored for anomalies
Current Controls: Logs collected but no automated anomaly detection
Control Recommendations: 1. Implement SIEM or log analysis tool (e.g., Splunk, ELK); 2. Configure alerts for suspicious patterns (multiple failed logins, unusual data access); 3. Weekly log review process; 4. Integrate with incident response

Current Controls: Automated backups enabled but restoration not tested
Control Recommendations: 1. Monthly backup restoration drill (already planned in resilience docs); 2. Document actual RTO/RPO achieved; 3. Automate restoration testing; 4. Test PITR to specific timestamp

Risk Title: Audit Log Deletion by Malicious Actor
Control Recommendations: 1. Verify retention lock is active; 2. Monitor for any attempts to delete audit logs; 3. Secondary log backup to separate GCS bucket; 4. Annual retention lock verification

Threat Event: Man-in-the-middle attack intercepting ePHI in transit
Control Recommendations: 1. Verify TLS 1.3 is preferred; 2. Remove TLS 1.0/1.1 support; 3. Enable HSTS with preload; 4. Quarterly SSL Labs scan
1. Enable GitHub Dependency Review; 2. Pin dependency versions (no wildcards); 3. Use private PyPI mirror for critical deps; 4. Quarterly dependency security audit
1. Multi-region backup storage; 2. Document multi-region failover procedure; 3. Annual DR drill (already planned); 4. Consider multi-region deployment for critical services

Threat Event: Weak password policy allows easy guessing
Control Recommendations: 1. Enforce strong password policy (12+ chars, complexity); 2. Password breach detection (haveibeenpwned); 3. Force password reset every 90 days; 4. Prevent password reuse

Threat Event: Supply chain attack via vulnerable container image
Control Recommendations: 1. Enable GCR Vulnerability Scanning; 2. Block deployment of images with critical CVEs; 3. Scan all base images before use; 4. Quarterly image audit

Risk Title: Verbose Error Messages Exposing System Details
Threat Event: Verbose error messages exposing internal details
Vulnerability: Verbose error messages exposing system details
Control Recommendations: 1. Review all API error responses; 2. Ensure 500 errors return generic message; 3. Log detailed errors server-side only; 4. Remove stack traces from production responses
1. Code review for dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage; 2. Content Security Policy (CSP) headers; 3. Automated XSS testing in CI/CD; 4. Annual penetration test

Current Controls: JWT validation on all protected endpoints
Control Recommendations: 1. Automated tests for auth bypass attempts; 2. Review all API endpoints for missing auth; 3. Implement API gateway with centralized auth; 4. Penetration test findings review

Current Controls: Cloud Armor enabled with basic DDoS rules
Control Recommendations: 1. Configure OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set; 2. Enable geo-blocking for high-risk countries; 3. Rate limiting per IP; 4. Monthly WAF rule review
